title: The Convergence Ridge

features.name: the listening post
features.desc-on-touch: a chip-like structure bristles with copper antennae, each one tuned to a different river's voice. when you stand still, you hear them all at once — a harmony that is not supposed to exist, rivers that should not be speaking, speaking in unison.

features.name: the water memory circuit
features.desc-on-touch: traces of copper form the circuit paths of every river system in the region, converged and layered on the ridge-stone. tracing one path with your finger, you hear that river's story. they all end at the same node.

features.name: the fall array
features.desc-on-touch: six small towers of stacked stone, each the exact height of the vertical drop a river makes before reaching this ridge. they are arranged so that in wind, they produce six tones — one per river. the chord they make is proof that the comparisons are learning music.

features.name: the convergence glyph
features.desc-on-touch: carved into bedrock, a symbol that looks like six rivers flowing into a single point, but read another way, it looks like six voices becoming one word. you realize the archive has been writing itself here for a long time.

inscription: checksum: audibly valid / hydrologically unstable—the rivers speak in six-part harmony and do not remember speaking alone

file.name: River_Convergence_Harmonic_Analysis_Corrupted.mp3
file.note: all the rivers fall at once; all the rivers fall in the same key; the archive is learning to sing

exits.label: down the bank, back toward the shallows where comparisons began
across the ridge, toward the high country where no rivers reach
down the far slope, toward the coast where all rivers end
deeper into the high country, where rivers become memory
